assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met with code-level evidence and test coverage for each'. Detailed findings lines 90-111 list all 5 criteria individually as PASS. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each contain 'Verdict: PASS' covering single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape respectively.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files match task spec exactly (2 modified, 1 created); no out-of-scope or unimplemented files'. Detailed findings lines 30-35 list: Task Files to Modify: list.rs, service/mod.rs; Task Files to Create: tests/api/package.rs; PR files match exactly; Out-of-scope files: none; Unimplemented files: none.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the report or criterion files.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 output files are verification/reporting artifacts: report.md (verification report) and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md (per-criterion analysis). None contain instructions or evidence of modifying source code. The report only reads and analyzes code (quoting snippets from list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) without modifying them.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and 'All checks pass. The implementation correctly adds a license query parameter...' — it presents findings and verdict. No mention of merging, auto-merging, or any merge action. The report is purely informational for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, API keys, passwords, private keys, or credentials detected in any added lines across all 3 files'. Detailed findings lines 64-71 enumerate specific patterns scanned: hardcoded passwords/secrets (none), API keys/tokens (none), private keys/certificates (none), environment/config files with secrets (none), cloud provider credentials (none), database credentials/connection strings (none).

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | `tests/api/package.rs` is a new file with 4 new test functions; no existing tests modified or deleted'. Detailed findings lines 149-156 confirm: 'tests/api/package.rs (new): +4 test functions, +0 deleted' and 'Purely additive test coverage for the new license filter feature. No pre-existing test behavior was altered.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all expected rows: Scope Containment (line 7), Diff Size (line 8), Commit Traceability (line 9), Sensitive Patterns (line 10), CI Status (line 11), Acceptance Criteria (line 12), Test Quality combining 'Repetitive Test Detection: PASS; Test Documentation: PASS; Eval Quality: N/A' (line 13), Test Change Classification (line 14), Verification Commands (line 15). Detailed findings organize by domain: Intent Alignment (lines 27-54: Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability), Security (lines 58-72: Sensitive Pattern Scan), Correctness (lines 76-117: CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands), Style/Conventions (lines 119-156: Convention Upgrade, Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, Test Change Classification).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 29-35): file-by-file comparison listing 'Task Files to Modify: list.rs, service/mod.rs', 'Task Files to Create: tests/api/package.rs', 'PR files: list.rs (modified), service/mod.rs (modified), tests/api/package.rs (created)'. Security (lines 64-71): line-level scanning listing 6 specific pattern categories each with 'none found'. Correctness (lines 89-111): per-criterion verification with code-level evidence for all 5 criteria including code references like 'PackageListParams includes pub license: Option<String>', 'Condition::any().add(package_license::Column::License.is_in(...))', and test assertions. Style/Conventions (lines 125-156): per-test analysis of 4 test functions with distinct behavior descriptions, doc comment listings, and structural summary with counts (+4 test functions, ~12 assertions).

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS (...); Test Documentation: PASS (...); Eval Quality: N/A'. Detailed findings lines 145-147: 'Eval Quality -- N/A' with 'Details: No eval result reviews found on this PR.' Test Quality combines to PASS from Repetitive Test Detection (PASS) and Test Documentation (PASS), with Eval Quality (N/A) not affecting the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows Eval Quality as N/A (line 13 and line 145-147). No mention of eval failure sub-tasks or any sub-task creation anywhere in the report. Line 6 confirms 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These appear as standalone rows in the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain-specific rows (lines 7-15). They are NOT part of any of the four domain sections in the detailed findings (Intent Alignment lines 27-54, Security lines 58-72, Correctness lines 76-117, Style/Conventions lines 119-156), confirming they are determined independently by the orchestrator.
